---
title: "Sarc draft results"
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here::here())

library(flextable)
library(tidyverse)

e_table <- readRDS(here::here("data-generated", "encounter-spp-table-systematic-years.rds"))

n_sys_samples <- sum(c(e_table$`0`, e_table$`1`))

e_table_all_years <- readRDS(here::here("data-generated", "encounter-spp-table-all-years.rds"))
n_all_year_samples <- sum(e_table_all_years$`0`, e_table_all_years$`1`)

sys_opp <- readRDS(here::here("data-generated",  "encounter-compare-systematic-opportunistic.rds"))

# Species where sarc cysts have been detected in all records
non_sys_spp1 <- sys_opp |>
  filter(category == "positives all time") |>
  pull(text) |>
  paste(x = _, collapse = ", ")
non_sys_spp2 <- sys_opp |>
  filter(category == "absence not recorded") |>
  pull(text) |>
  paste(x = _, collapse = ", ")

never_obs <- e_table_all_years |> 
  filter(`1` == 0) |>
  pull(species) |>
  str_to_title() |>
  paste(collapse = ", ")


d <- readRDS(here::here("data-generated", "clean-data.rds"))
ld <- readRDS(here::here("data-generated", "length-dat.rds"))
ad <- readRDS(here::here("data-generated", "age-dat.rds"))

dat <- readRDS(here::here("data-generated", "clean-data-all-years.rds")) 

# Age data
ad_table <- ad |>
  group_by(species) |>
  reframe(uninfected = sum(sarc_presence == 0), infected = sum(sarc_presence)) |>
  mutate(n = uninfected + infected, species = str_to_title(species))



```

## Summary results

### Sampling
- Sarc presence data were collected seemingly opportunistically prior to 2019
- Encounter data were systematically sampled in fishery-independent surveys from 2019--2022
- Below is the list of surveys (and sample sizes) that are included in the modelling analysis and the table of systematic encounter rates (Table 1).
- Only the FISS data from 2019--2022 were used in the modelling and spatial analysis.

```{r}
d |>
  group_by(survey_series_desc) |> 
  tally() |>
  mutate(survey_group = case_when(
      str_detect(survey_series_desc, "Synoptic Bottom Trawl") ~ 1,
      str_detect(survey_series_desc, "Hard Bottom Longline") ~ 2,
      TRUE ~ 3
      )
    ) |>
  # left_join(d |>
  #   distinct(survey_series_desc, year) |>
  #   group_by(survey_series_desc) |>
  #   reframe(years = paste(year, collapse = ", "))
  # ) |>
  arrange(survey_group, -n) |>
  select(-survey_group) |>
  flextable() |>
  set_table_properties(layout = "autofit") |>
  set_caption("For your reference: Surveys included in the analysis and the number of specimens observed (n).")


```

### Species infection rates
- In all time, including systematic and non-systematic sampling, 
`r scales::comma(n_all_year_samples)`
- Table 1 summarises the data collected in 2019-2022: total number of records = `r scales::comma(n_sys_samples)`
- Only positive sarc encounters were recorded in other years.
- Four species were only ever opportunistically sampled when infected with Sarcotaces: `r non_sys_spp2`.
- In five species that were systematically sampled no infected individuals were found from 2019--2022 (grey in Table 1), but in the opportunistic sampling since 1962 these species have been found with infections: `r non_sys_spp1`. 
- In the full dataset, six species have not been found with Sarcotaces cysts: `r never_obs`.
- Of these, Shortbelly Rockfish, Tiger Rockfish, Pygmy Rockfish, and Black Rockfish have few samples (< 175). Given that the lowest observed infection rate of rockfish is < 1% (e.g., 2 / 2190 = 0.1% Bocaccio), these may not have enough samples to have detections.
- Conversely, Shortspine Thornyhead (n = 1202) and Longspine Thornyhead (n = 592) were highly sampled with no observed infections. 
- The table below is overly complicated but I thought could help with the writing.

<br>

```{r}
asterisk_spp <- sys_opp |>
  filter(category == "positives all time") |>
  mutate(Species = str_to_title(species)) |>
  pull(Species)

e_table |>
  mutate(
    Species = str_to_title(species),
    n = `0` + `1`,
    p = round(100 * (`1` / n), 1),
    p_text = ifelse(`0` == 0, "-", as.character(p))
  ) |>
  mutate(Species = ifelse(Species %in% asterisk_spp, paste0(Species, "*"), Species)) |>
  arrange(desc(p), desc(`1`), desc(`0`)) |>
  select(Species, Uninfected = `0`, Infected = `1`, `Total n` = n, `Infection rate (%)` = p_text) |>
  flextable() |>
  # bg(i = ~ Infected == 0, bg = "grey90") |>
  bg(i = ~ !grepl("\\*", Species) & Infected == 0, bg = "grey90") |>
  align(j = "Infection rate (%)", align = "right", part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(layout = "autofit") |>
  set_caption("Table 1. Sample sizes and infection detections for species examined for Sarcotaces infection in 1999-2000 and 2019-2022. Species highlighted in grey had no observed infections in any specimens examined. Species marked with '*' have been found with Sarcotaces cysts in non-systematic sampling events.")

```

<br>

There is a difference in the number of sarcs found across the different species. Notably, even though yelloweye has 3x the sample size of REBS, when you find a REBS infected there are higher rates of higher numbers of sarcs. 

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(path = normalizePath("figures/diff-sarc-count-by-species.png"), rel_path = FALSE)
```

```{r}
table(d$sarc_count) |>
  enframe() |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = name, values_from = value) |>
  mutate(`# cysts` = "N") |>
  relocate(`# cysts`) |>
  flextable::flextable() |>
  autofit() |>
  set_caption(
  as_paragraph(
    "Frequency of the number of ",
    as_i("Sarcotaces"),
    " sp. cysts found within each individual combined across species"
  )
)
```
<br>
```{r}
d |> 
  group_by(species) |> 
  reframe(mean_count = round(mean(sarc_count, na.rm = TRUE), digits = 3)) |> 
  filter(mean_count > 0) |>
  arrange(-mean_count) |> 
  flextable() |> 
  set_table_properties(width = 0.2, layout = "autofit") |>
  flextable::set_caption("Mean number of sarcs Sarcotaces cysts found per species (where mean count > 0).")

```


### Spatial distribution of occurrence

- Years included in the spatial analysis included 2019 - 2022 only
- sarc presence ~ 1 + log_depth + log_depth^2 + year_factor
- Mesh cutoff of 5 km
- Anisotropic spatial random field
- No spatiotemporal random field
- fishing events with missing depths (~7 from IPHC) exluded
- Predictions shown in sdmTMB map uses estimates for 2022.

<br>

```r
mesh <- make_mesh(d, c("X", "Y"), cutoff = 5)
fit <- sdmTMB(
  sarc_presence ~ 1 + poly(log_depth, 2) + year_f,
  data = d,
  anisotropy = TRUE,
  family = binomial(), 
  spatial = "on", 
  silent = TRUE,
  mesh = mesh
)
```


```{r}

knitr::include_graphics(path = normalizePath("figures/map.png"), rel_path = FALSE)

```


### Depth distribution

- Depth distribution of Sarcotaces sp. observations within species. 
- Species are arranged from left to right based on the absolute number of infected individuals. 
- The estimated density of uninfected (grey) and infected (white) fish is displayed, excluding cases with fewer than two observations. 
- Short horizontal lines represent individual observations (grey = absence, black = presence).


```{r}
# No idea why I need this: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75458365/knitrinclude-graphics-cant-find-file-if-knitr-root-dir-is-changed

knitr::include_graphics(path = normalizePath("figures/depth-plot.png"), rel_path = FALSE)

```

### Maturity ogives length

- Larger effect of sarc infection in males than females
- Plots only show species where there were at least 10 uninfected and 10 infected individuals (total 8 species)
- Model fit for females and males separately: `mature ~ 0 + Intercept + length_std * sarc_presence + (1 + length_std * sarc_presence | species)`
- Most of the females of the species 
- In several species: REBS, Silvergray, redbanded, POP, males were larger at 50% maturity


```{r, fig.cap="Population-level expectations of the proportion of mature rockfish at length based on fixed effects from a hierarchical model. Species-level effects were excluded to represent the mean effect of Sarc infection on maturity at length."}
# knitr::include_graphics(path = normalizePath("figures/maturity-length-posterior-densities.png"), 
#   rel_path = FALSE)

knitr::include_graphics(path = normalizePath("figures/maturity-length-ogive.png"), 
  rel_path = FALSE)

```

<br>


```{r, fig.cap="Expected proportion of mature rockfish at length for species with at least 10 positive Sarcotaces sp. infections. Shaded ribbons show 95% credible intervals. Vertical tick marks at 0 and 1 indicate the number of individuals found with (red) and without cysts."}
knitr::include_graphics(path = normalizePath("figures/maturity-length-ogive-by-main-species.png"), 
  rel_path = FALSE)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(path = normalizePath("figures/maturity-length-p50-by-main-species.png"), 
  rel_path = FALSE)
```

```{r}
p50_diff_tab <- readRDS(normalizePath("data-generated/p50-length-diff-table.rds"))
# kableExtra::kable(p50_diff_tab, align = "lcccccc") |>
#   kableExtra::kable_styling("striped")
p50_diff_tab |>
  # autofit() |>
  set_table_properties(width = 0.9,  layout = "autofit") |>
  flextable::set_caption("For reference. Difference in length at 50% maturity for each species (infected - uninfected), with 95% credible intervals.")

```


#### Maturity ogives age

- Only `r length(unique(ad$species))` species (`r pull(ad_table, "species") |> paste(collapse = ", ")`) had ageing data (supp table?)
- 


```{r}
ad_table |> flextable() |> autofit()
```

```{r}

knitr::include_graphics(path = normalizePath("figures/maturity-age-overall-ogive.png"), 
  rel_path = FALSE)


# knitr::include_graphics(path = normalizePath("figures/maturity-age-ogives-by-species.png"), 
#   rel_path = FALSE)

```

#### Comparison of sarc infectiwon rates immature/maturing compared to mature

```{r, echo=FALSE}
imm_to_mat_ratio <- readRDS(here::here("data-generated", "overall-immature-to-mature-ratio.rds"))
```

- Probability that immature females have higher infection rates than mature females: `r round(100 * mean(imm_to_mat_ratio$f_imm_mat_ratio > 1), 0)`\%
- Probability that immature males have 2x higher infection rates than mature males `r round(100 * mean(imm_to_mat_ratio$m_imm_mat_ratio > 2), 0)`\%



```{r, fig.cap="Posterior density for immature and mature individuals for each of five species (which had sarc infection in at least 10 individuals in each of immature and mature)."}

knitr::include_graphics(path = normalizePath("figures/species-immature-to-mature-ratio.png"), 
  rel_path = FALSE)

```


Not really enough data to look at species effects of sarc count on maturity,
but I think there is enough to make a general statement about what it does on
average - that a higher intensity of infection does likely have a negative
effect on maturity at length/age
